name: Mongolian People's Republic
note: Established as a Soviet satellite state after Soviet-backed revolution against the theocratic Bogd Khanate. The purges of the 1930s under Choibalsan (the 'Mongolian Stalin') massacred 30,000–35,000 people, mostly Buddhist monks — 700 monasteries destroyed. Mongolia was a loyal Soviet ally and the second-oldest communist state. Transitioned to democracy in 1990 and formally dissolved in 1992.

figures.name: Damdin Sükhbaatar
figures.significance: Revolutionary hero; led the Mongolian revolution with Soviet support; died young and possibly poisoned; national hero whose statue dominates Ulaanbaatar's central square

figures.name: Khorloogiin Choibalsan
figures.significance: The 'Mongolian Stalin'; collectivised herds, destroyed Buddhism, executed tens of thousands; also the general who defeated Japan at Khalkhin Gol (1939) alongside Zhukov
